questions: Identify the specific Apple Online Store Sales & Refund Policy for the region where refunds are explicitly restricted to being remitted only to a domestic bank account where the holder's name matches the payor. Based on this regional document, provide a comprehensive summary of the return conditions for the following three categories: (1) iPhones (specifically regarding wireless service agreements), (2) 'Volume' purchases (defining the quantity threshold and applicable restocking fee), and (3) ZEISS Optical Inserts (contrasting the return location eligibility for Prescription versions versus Readers).

Identify the specific macOS version that introduces 'Apple Intelligence' and the ability to view and control a paired iPhone directly from the desktop. Based on the user guide documentation for this release, describe the new capabilities provided for Spotlight and communication, the specific security mechanism for handling deleted credentials, and the guidance offered for users transitioning from a Windows PC.

According to Apple's Singapore sales policies and the Apple Intelligence feature availability documentation, how does the eligibility for returning ZEISS Optical Inserts to a physical Apple Store location differ between the 'Prescription' and 'Readers' models? Additionally, is the 'Live Translation with AirPods' feature available to a user with a Singapore Apple Account while traveling in the European Union, and what specific residency condition determines this access?

According to the Mac User Guide's overview of new features and device integration, identify the specific macOS version that introduces 'automatic translations in calls and messages' alongside a 'fresh new design'. For this specific operating system, explain how it allows users to interact with their iPhone directly from the desktop and describe the capabilities mentioned regarding password management and recovery.

Identify the specific macOS version that introduces the capability to 'view and control your iPhone directly from your Mac desktop' and includes 'Apple Intelligence' integration. Using the user guide for this operating system, summarize the resources available for users transitioning from a Windows PC, detail the new interactive capabilities of the Spotlight search tool, and explain the specific provision for restoring lost authentication credentials.

rounds.question: Identify the specific Apple Online Store Sales & Refund Policy for the region where refunds are explicitly restricted to being remitted only to a domestic bank account where the holder's name matches the payor. Based on this regional document, provide a comprehensive summary of the return conditions for the following three categories: (1) iPhones (specifically regarding wireless service agreements), (2) 'Volume' purchases (defining the quantity threshold and applicable restocking fee), and (3) ZEISS Optical Inserts (contrasting the return location eligibility for Prescription versions versus Readers).
checklist_width: iPhone Return Detail: Returning an iPhone does not automatically cancel/reset the wireless account; the user is responsible for the agreement and fees.
Volume Purchase Definition: Aggregate of more than 4 items per order or across multiple orders from the same product category.
Volume Purchase Penalty: Subject to a 25% restocking fee per item (and must be returned within 7 days).
ZEISS Inserts (Prescription): Custom third-party products; cannot be returned to an Apple Store location (must return online/via phone).
ZEISS Inserts (Readers): Can be returned to an Apple Store location (or online/via phone).
checklist_depth: Target Region: Singapore (identified via the 'domestic bank account matching payor' refund clause).

Logic Proof: The Singapore Consumer Store policy is the only one containing the specific text: 'We will only remit refunds to a Singapore bank account where the name of the bank account holder matches the payor’s name'.
rounds.rationale: The question requires **Deep Reasoning** to identify the correct document (Apple Singapore Sales Policy) by filtering for a unique, obscure financial clause regarding refund remittances found in the source text. It then demands **Wide Aggregation** by requiring the agent to extract and synthesize rules for three distinct product categories (iPhones, Volume purchases, and ZEISS Inserts) that are scattered across different sections of that single policy document.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: macOS Support Reset your Apple ID password If you forgot your Apple ID password, you can reset it and regain access to your account. Learn how to reset your password Reinstall
rounds.answer_a:
Apple's Online Store Sales and Refund Policy for China explicitly restricts refunds to be remitted only to a domestic bank account where the account holder's name matches the original payor, ensuring compliance with local financial regulations [1]. For category (1), iPhones purchased in China are subject to wireless service agreements that may impact return eligibility; however, Apple allows returns within 14 days of purchase if the device is unused and in original condition, regardless of carrier attachment, provided all components are included [1]. Customers must deactivate Activation Lock and retain original packaging [1]. 

Regarding category (2), “Volume” purchases refer to orders of 12 or more units of the same product, which are considered bulk transactions. These are subject to a restocking fee of up to 15% if returned, and such purchases require prior authorization from Apple Sales Support. Returns must be initiated within 14 days and items must be unopened and in new condition [1]. 

For category (3), ZEISS Optical Inserts have differing return policies based on type. Prescription ZEISS Optical Inserts, being custom-made, are not eligible for return or exchange except in cases of manufacturing defects. In contrast, non-prescription Readers (ready-made lenses) can be returned within 14 days if unused and in original packaging. Only inserts purchased directly from the Apple Online Store in China can be returned via mail; third-party or retail store purchases must be returned to the point of purchase. Prescription inserts cannot be returned to Apple retail stores even within China due to customization [1].

## References  
[1] Apple China Online Store - Sales and Refunds Policy, https://www.apple.com/cn/legal/sales-support/refunds/
rounds.answer_b:
The Apple Online Store region with refunds restricted to domestic bank accounts matching the payor’s name is China [1]. Return conditions: 1. iPhones (wireless service agreements): 14-day return window from receipt; devices must be unused/unopened (or minimally used with no damage) and unlocked from the associated carrier [1]. You must cancel the wireless service agreement before requesting a device refund—failing to do so results in a partial refund deducting unfulfilled service costs [1]. 2. Volume purchases: Defined as orders with ≥10 units per product [1]; a 15% restocking fee applies, and customized volume orders are non-returnable [1]. Returns require original packaging and proof of purchase [1]. 3. ZEISS Optical Inserts: Prescription versions are only eligible for online returns (no in-store drop-offs) within 14 days, with unopened/unused condition and original packaging [2]. Readers are eligible for both online and in-store returns within the same 14-day window, though in-store returns need valid proof of purchase to confirm online order linkage [2]. All returns must meet these criteria to qualify for refund processing to the payor’s matching domestic bank account [1].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. (2024). Apple Online Store Sales & Refund Policy (China). Retrieved from https://www.apple.com.cn/legal/sales_policies/online.html
[2] Apple Inc. (2024). ZEISS Optical Inserts Product Return Guidelines (China). Retrieved from https://www.apple.com.cn/shop/accessories/all-accessories/zeiss-optical-inserts
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents failed the DEEP Logic check by incorrectly identifying the target region as China. The Ground Truth explicitly states that the specific refund clause (remitting only to a domestic bank account matching the payor's name) identifies the region as Singapore. Because both agents selected the wrong region, they consequently failed the WIDE Aggregation check, providing incorrect details for the 'Volume' purchase category (citing thresholds of 10-12 units and 15% fees, whereas the Singapore policy specifies >4 units and a 25% fee). While Agent A offered significantly better formatting (Agent B presented a difficult-to-read wall of text), the shared fundamental failure to identify the correct entity and policy document results in a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.question: Identify the specific macOS version that introduces 'Apple Intelligence' and the ability to view and control a paired iPhone directly from the desktop. Based on the user guide documentation for this release, describe the new capabilities provided for Spotlight and communication, the specific security mechanism for handling deleted credentials, and the guidance offered for users transitioning from a Windows PC.
checklist_width: Spotlight: Ability to search and take action without opening apps
Communication: Availability of automatic translations in calls and messages
Security: Ability to recover recently deleted passwords or passkeys
Switching from Windows: Guidance on transferring files from a PC and finding your way around the Mac
checklist_depth: Target Entity: macOS Sequoia (or 'Tahoe' as referenced in the source text)
Logic Proof: Identified as the OS featuring 'Apple Intelligence' and iPhone Mirroring/Desktop Control
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify the specific OS version (Sequoia/Tahoe) based on its unique features (Apple Intelligence, iPhone Mirroring) rather than a direct name search. It then demands Wide Aggregation by requesting details from four distinct sections of the source text: 'What’s new' (Spotlight/Communication), 'Privacy' (Passwords), and 'Switching' (Windows transition).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Get started with your Mac You can set up and start using your new Mac in no time. Choose a wallpaper for your desktop, change common settings, start using apps and more. Learn
rounds.answer_a:
Apple Intelligence and the ability to view and control a paired iPhone directly from the macOS desktop were introduced in macOS Sequoia (version 15.0) [1]. This release integrates deeply with iPhone through the new iPhone Mirroring feature, allowing users to access their entire iPhone interface, including apps and notifications, directly on their Mac, with changes synced seamlessly between devices [2].

Spotlight gains enhanced natural language understanding and broader system integration, enabling more contextual and conversational queries, such as finding files based on activity or time, and pulling results from Mail, Messages, and third-party apps with greater accuracy [1]. In communication, macOS Sequoia introduces Writing Tools powered by Apple Intelligence, offering proofreading, summarization, and text rewriting across apps like Mail and Notes, streamlining content creation [3].

For security, when users delete saved passwords or credentials in Safari, macOS Sequoia implements on-device cryptographic erasure, ensuring data is irreversibly removed from iCloud Keychain across all devices using end-to-end encryption protocols [2].

The user guide provides a dedicated "Switching from Windows" section, advising users to leverage Continuity features like Handoff, Universal Clipboard, and iCloud Drive for seamless file access. It also recommends signing in with Apple ID as the first step, using Migration Assistant for data transfer, and adopting built-in apps like Mail and Safari to maximize ecosystem integration [3].

## References  
[1] https://support.apple.com/en-us/117746  
[2] https://www.apple.com/macos/sequoia-preview/  
[3] https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/transition-from-windows-mchl67d08c89/mac
rounds.answer_b:
macOS Sonoma 14 introduces Apple Intelligence and direct iPhone desktop viewing/control [1]. For Spotlight, it supports natural language queries to summarize documents, calculate categorized expenses, and pull context from spreadsheets, letting users skip manual data sifting [2]. Communication upgrades include context-aware message/email auto-complete, tone-matched smart replies, and draft summarization to speed up edits for time-sensitive conversations [2]. Deleted credentials use end-to-end encrypted temporary storage (30-day retention) with zero Apple or third-party access during this window, followed by permanent, irrecoverable deletion to prevent unauthorized retrieval [3]. Windows transition guidance covers a dedicated transfer tool for files, settings, and compatible apps, a shortcut tutorial for macOS workflows, and a support hub to address compatibility issues with Windows-exclusive software [4]. This bundle of features balances productivity boosts with robust data security and seamless cross-platform adaptation.

## References
1. Apple Inc. "macOS Sonoma 14 Official Release Notes." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT213816.
2. Apple Inc. "macOS Sonoma User Guide: Spotlight and Communication Enhancements." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/guide/macos/spotlight-mchlp1063/mac.
3. Apple Inc. "macOS Sonoma Security Overview: Credential Management." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/guide/security/credential-protection-sec7c679f3c9/mac.
4. Apple Inc. "Windows to macOS Transition Guide." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/guide/macos/transfer-data-from-windows-computer-mchlp1540/mac.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the target entity (macOS Sequoia) and provided mostly relevant details regarding Apple Intelligence and iPhone Mirroring. However, it failed the specific 'Security' sub-point by claiming deleted credentials are 'irreversibly removed,' whereas the actual feature (and Ground Truth) highlights the ability to *recover* recently deleted passwords in the new Passwords app. Agent B failed the fundamental Deep Logic check by identifying the wrong OS (macOS Sonoma), making its subsequent details factually inapplicable.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

rounds.question: According to Apple's Singapore sales policies and the Apple Intelligence feature availability documentation, how does the eligibility for returning ZEISS Optical Inserts to a physical Apple Store location differ between the 'Prescription' and 'Readers' models? Additionally, is the 'Live Translation with AirPods' feature available to a user with a Singapore Apple Account while traveling in the European Union, and what specific residency condition determines this access?
checklist_width: ZEISS Prescription Inserts: Cannot be returned to a physical Apple Store location (must use online/phone return).
ZEISS Readers Inserts: Can be returned to a physical Apple Store location.
Live Translation with AirPods: Yes, it is available/functional for the Singapore user in the EU.
Condition: The restriction applies only to EU residents whose Apple Account Region is also in the EU; users from other regions can use it while traveling.
checklist_depth: Logic Proof 1: Correctly identifies the distinction in return methods based on the specific product sub-type (Prescription vs. Readers) found in the Singapore Store policy.
Logic Proof 2: Correctly applies the 'Traveler vs. Resident' logic to the EU restriction, determining that a Singapore account holder is exempt from the EU block.
rounds.rationale: This question requires 'Deep' reasoning to parse two specific conditional logic gates: the distinction between two sub-types of the same accessory (ZEISS) regarding return locations, and the double-condition (Location + Account Region) required to trigger the EU feature block. It is 'Wide' because the agent must aggregate information from the Store Policy section (Target 0) and the Technical Feature Availability section (Target 1) to provide a complete answer.
source_nodes: How to get Apple Intelligence - Apple Support
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Singapore Consumer Store Thanks for shopping at Apple. We appreciate the fact that you like to buy the cool stuff we build. We also want to make sure you have a rewarding expe
rounds.answer_a:
ZEISS Optical Inserts for the 'Prescription' model of Apple Vision Pro are eligible for return to a physical Apple Store in Singapore only if the frames have not been fitted with personalized lenses and remain in resalable condition; once customized with prescription lenses, they are considered final sale and cannot be returned, as outlined in Apple’s Singapore-specific sales policy [1]. In contrast, the 'Readers' model, which features non-prescription magnification for near-vision tasks, is treated as a standard accessory and qualifies for return within 14 days of purchase, provided the item is unused and in original packaging [1]. This distinction reflects Apple’s differentiation between medical-grade customized products and off-the-shelf accessories under Singapore’s consumer protection guidelines.

The 'Live Translation with AirPods' feature, part of Apple’s Apple Intelligence suite, is available to users with a Singapore Apple Account while traveling in the European Union, but only if the feature is enabled based on the user’s primary residency setting in their Apple ID account [2]. According to Apple’s feature availability documentation, access to Apple Intelligence services, including Live Translation, is determined by the user’s selected country or region of residency—not their current physical location [2]. As long as the user's Apple ID residency is set to Singapore and Singapore is listed as a supported region for the feature, access continues uninterrupted abroad. However, if the residency is changed to a non-supported region, the service may be automatically disabled. Apple confirms that features synced via iCloud, like Live Translation, remain active during international travel under these conditions [2].

## References  
[1] https://www.apple.com/sg/legal/sales-support/  
[2] https://support.apple.com/en-sg/123456-apple-intelligence-availability
rounds.answer_b:
Under Apple’s Singapore sales policies, ZEISS Optical Inserts’ physical Apple Store return eligibility differs sharply between Prescription and Readers models [1]. Prescription inserts, custom-fitted to individual eye prescriptions, only qualify for returns within 14 days if defective; non-defective custom Prescription inserts cannot be returned or exchanged, as they are tailored to unique user needs [1]. Conversely, off-the-shelf Readers models allow 14-day returns for any reason if unopened and in original packaging; opened Readers are eligible for returns only if defective, with proof of purchase required for all returns [1]. For Live Translation with AirPods, a user with a Singapore Apple Account traveling in the European Union can access the feature [2]. The specific residency condition determining this access is that the user’s Singapore Apple Account must have a primary billing address registered to a Singapore residential address, validating their Singapore residency; the feature also requires compatible AirPods (Pro, Max, or 3rd-gen) and device software updated to macOS Sonoma 14, iOS 17, or later [2]. This residency check aligns with regional feature rollout parameters in Apple Intelligence availability documentation, ensuring access is tied to verified Singapore residency regardless of current travel location [2].

## References
1. Apple Inc. "Singapore Sales and Returns Policy: ZEISS Optical Inserts." Apple Singapore, 2024, https://www.apple.com/sg/legal/sales_policies/#zeiss-optical-inserts.
2. Apple Inc. "Apple Intelligence Feature Availability: Live Translation with AirPods." Apple Support, 2024, https://support.apple.com/en-sg/HT214568.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed the primary logic test regarding the ZEISS Optical Inserts return policy (Logic Proof 1). The Ground Truth establishes that Prescription inserts *can* be returned, but must be processed online rather than at a physical store. 

- **Agent A** incorrectly claimed that Prescription inserts are "final sale" and cannot be returned at all once customized.
- **Agent B** incorrectly claimed that Prescription inserts can only be returned "if defective".

Both agents hallucinated a restrictive non-return policy instead of identifying the correct distinction (Online vs. In-Store returns). While both agents correctly answered the second part of the query regarding EU travel and Apple Intelligence (Logic Proof 2), the significant factual error on the first constraint results in a Low Quality Tie. Agent A had slightly better formatting (paragraphs), but accuracy is paramount.

rounds.question: According to the Mac User Guide's overview of new features and device integration, identify the specific macOS version that introduces 'automatic translations in calls and messages' alongside a 'fresh new design'. For this specific operating system, explain how it allows users to interact with their iPhone directly from the desktop and describe the capabilities mentioned regarding password management and recovery.
checklist_width: Integration: View and control iPhone directly from Mac desktop
Integration: Works seamlessly with other Apple devices (copy and paste mentioned)
Privacy: Includes built-in password management tools
Privacy: Ability to recover deleted passwords or passkeys
Feature: Use Spotlight to search and take action without opening apps (contextual detail)
checklist_depth: Logic Proof: Identified via the unique combination of 'fresh new design' and 'automatic translations in calls and messages' found in the 'What’s new' section.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify the specific OS ('macOS Tahoe') using unique feature descriptors (automatic translations, fresh design) without naming it. It then requires Wide Aggregation to pull specific details about iPhone mirroring (found in the 'Use your Mac with other devices' section) and password recovery (found in the 'Control your privacy' section), which are distinct from the 'What's new' section.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Get started with your Mac You can set up and start using your new Mac in no time. Choose a wallpaper for your desktop, change common settings, start using apps and more. Learn
rounds.answer_a:
The specific macOS version that introduces automatic translations in calls and messages alongside a fresh new design is macOS Sequoia (15.0) [1]. This release features a redesigned user interface with refined menu bars, control centers, and window management, emphasizing visual clarity and seamless integration across Apple devices [2]. A standout capability is the integration of Apple Intelligence to enable real-time, on-device translation during FaceTime audio calls and in Messages, supporting multiple languages without requiring third-party apps [1].

Users can interact with their iPhone directly from the Mac desktop via the iPhone Mirroring feature, which allows full access to the iPhone’s interface, including apps, settings, and notifications, all within a window on the Mac. Actions taken on the Mac are mirrored live on the iPhone, maintaining synchronization across devices [2].

Regarding password management, macOS Sequoia enhances the built-in Passwords app with cross-platform autofill and secure sharing of credentials between trusted devices. It introduces Recovery Codes for iCloud Keychain, allowing users to regain access to saved passwords if their primary device is unavailable. Additionally, compromised passwords are automatically flagged, and users receive proactive suggestions for updating them across devices [3].

## References  
[1] https://support.apple.com/en-us/117746  
[2] https://www.apple.com/macos/sequoia-preview/  
[3] https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/manage-passwords-mchl7b1a74f0/mac
rounds.answer_b:
macOS Sequoia 15 introduces automatic translations in calls and messages alongside a fresh new design [1]. The translations function in real time for FaceTime and supported third-party calls (with editable outputs) and extend to iMessage exchanges (covering 17 initial languages), while the design overhauls Control Center, app icon styling, and window tiling layouts for streamlined navigation [1]. For iPhone desktop interaction, it expands integration by letting users launch iPhone apps directly on Mac, drag-and-drop files between devices without AirDrop, and manage iPhone notifications and incoming calls via macOS’s menu bar [2]. Password management adds auto-generated passkeys for legacy non-passkey-supporting apps, and a simplified recovery flow: users verify identity via a paired Apple Watch to restore lost passwords without a dedicated recovery key, reducing access barriers while upholding end-to-end encryption for all credential data [3].

## References
1. Apple Inc. "macOS Sequoia 15 Official Release Notes: New Features Overview." Apple Support, June 2024, https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT214225.
2. Apple Inc. "macOS Sequoia User Guide: iPhone Desktop Integration Tools." Apple Support, June 2024, https://support.apple.com/guide/macos/iphone-integration-mchlp2011/mac.
3. Apple Inc. "macOS Sequoia Security Guide: Password Management & Recovery Enhancements." Apple Support, June 2024, https://support.apple.com/guide/security/password-recovery-sec8d72f4b12/mac.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents failed the Deep Logic check by identifying the target entity as 'macOS Sequoia' instead of the Ground Truth's 'macOS Tahoe'. This suggests both agents relied on external knowledge of real-world features (iPhone Mirroring is a Sequoia feature) rather than the specific source text implied by the prompt, which seemingly combines these features with a different OS name and design description. Additionally, both agents failed the Wide Aggregation check regarding password management: the Ground Truth specifies the ability to 'recover deleted passwords' (a specific feature for undeleting credentials), whereas both agents discussed general account recovery (Recovery Codes/Apple Watch) for when a device is lost. While Agent A had significantly better formatting, the fundamental failure to identify the correct entity mandated by the Ground Truth results in a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.question: Identify the specific macOS version that introduces the capability to 'view and control your iPhone directly from your Mac desktop' and includes 'Apple Intelligence' integration. Using the user guide for this operating system, summarize the resources available for users transitioning from a Windows PC, detail the new interactive capabilities of the Spotlight search tool, and explain the specific provision for restoring lost authentication credentials.
checklist_width: Transitioning from Windows: Mentions learning to 'find your way around' the Mac.
Transitioning from Windows: Mentions how to 'transfer your files' from a PC.
Spotlight Capabilities: Ability to 'take action' directly from search results without opening apps.
Security/Credentials: Specific mention of the ability to 'recover a recently deleted password' or passkey.
checklist_depth: Target Entity: macOS Sequoia (or 'macOS Tahoe' if restricted strictly to the provided source text).
Logic Proof: The OS is identified by the unique feature of iPhone Mirroring ('View and control your iPhone directly from your Mac desktop') and Apple Intelligence integration.
rounds.rationale: The question uses Deep Reasoning by masking the specific OS name behind its unique feature set (iPhone Mirroring/Control and Apple Intelligence), forcing the agent to identify the correct version (conceptually macOS Sequoia, labeled 'Tahoe' in the source). It then applies Wide Aggregation by requesting specific, scattered details regarding three distinct user scenarios: switching from Windows, using Spotlight for actions, and recovering deleted passwords.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Get started with your Mac You can set up and start using your new Mac in no time. Choose a wallpaper for your desktop, change common settings, start using apps and more. Learn
rounds.answer_a:
macOS Sequoia (version 15.0) introduces the ability to view and control your iPhone directly from your Mac desktop, powered by iPhone Mirroring, and integrates Apple Intelligence across core apps and system functions [1]. This integration enables seamless interaction with the iPhone interface—apps, notifications, and settings—within a resizable window on the Mac, with real-time synchronization ensuring actions taken on one device reflect immediately on the other [2].

For users transitioning from Windows, macOS Sequoia’s user guide offers a dedicated “Switching from Windows” resource, recommending initial setup steps such as signing in with an Apple ID, using Migration Assistant to transfer files, and enabling iCloud sync for contacts, calendars, and mail [3]. It also encourages adoption of Continuity features—like Handoff, Universal Clipboard, and AirDrop—to streamline cross-device workflows [3].

Spotlight is significantly enhanced with Apple Intelligence, providing natural language understanding, deep system integration, and contextual awareness. Users can perform conversational searches—for example, locating files by activity or asking to “show photos from my trip last summer”—with results pulled from Mail, Messages, third-party apps, and system data [1].

For authentication recovery, macOS Sequoia introduces Recovery Codes for iCloud Keychain. If a user loses access to their trusted devices, these codes allow secure restoration of saved passwords and credentials, maintained through end-to-end encryption. Additionally, compromised passwords are automatically detected, prompting users to update them across devices [2].

## References  
[1] https://support.apple.com/en-us/117746  
[2] https://www.apple.com/macos/sequoia-preview/  
[3] https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/transition-from-windows-mchl67d08c89/mac
rounds.answer_b:
macOS Sonoma 14 introduces the capability to view and control a paired iPhone directly from the Mac desktop alongside Apple Intelligence integration [1]. For Windows transition, the user guide outlines three core resources: a dedicated transfer tool that moves files, system settings, and compatible apps to Mac without third-party software; a step-by-step shortcut tutorial to familiarize users with macOS workflows (e.g., window management, app launching); and a dedicated support hub that addresses compatibility gaps with Windows-exclusive software and offers personalized troubleshooting [2]. Spotlight’s new interactive capabilities include natural language query support to summarize long documents, calculate categorized expenses from spreadsheets, and extract context from tabular data, eliminating manual data sifting and speeding up task completion [3]. For lost authentication credential restoration, the guide specifies that users can restore lost credentials via iCloud Keychain by verifying identity with a paired trusted device (e.g., Apple Watch), with end-to-end encryption preserving data security; deleted credentials are stored in encrypted temporary storage (30-day retention, no Apple/third-party access) for possible restoration before permanent, irrecoverable deletion [4].

## References
1. Apple Inc. "macOS Sonoma 14 Official Release Notes." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT213816.
2. Apple Inc. "Windows to macOS Transition Guide." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/guide/macos/transfer-data-from-windows-computer-mchlp1540/mac.
3. Apple Inc. "macOS Sonoma User Guide: Spotlight Enhancements." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/guide/macos/spotlight-mchlp1063/mac.
4. Apple Inc. "macOS Sonoma Security Overview: Credential Management." Apple Support, September 2023, https://support.apple.com/guide/security/credential-protection-sec7c679f3c9/mac.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the operating system as macOS Sequoia (15), which introduced iPhone Mirroring and Apple Intelligence. Agent B failed the fundamental logic check by identifying macOS Sonoma (14), which does not support these features. However, Agent A is only 'Better' rather than 'Much Better' because it failed to accurately retrieve the specific details requested from the user guide regarding the sub-points. Specifically, it hallucinated 'Recovery Codes' for credential restoration instead of the correct 'Recently Deleted' folder in the new Passwords app, and it missed the specific 'take action' capability of Spotlight. Agent A also had superior formatting compared to Agent B's wall of text.
